meta.title: TURFu — Infrastructure for the next paradigm
meta.description: Transdisciplinary metastructure aligning ethics, collective intelligence, and decentralized technologies to transform human coordination.
meta.keywords: metaethics, governance, transparency, DAO, Web3, collective intelligence, transdisciplinarity

hero.tagline: An open research center rethinking how humanity validates, shares, and governs knowledge.
hero.subtitle: TURFu is a transdisciplinary research project. We produce analyses, build tools, and publish in open science — at the intersection of epistemology, ethics, AI, and decentralized technologies.

latestPublications.title: Recent publications
latestPublications.subtitle: Analyses, audits, specifications — our research is public.

ecosystem.title: What we build
ecosystem.intro: TURFu operates on three levels. A research framework (Layer 0) produces ethical and epistemic foundations. An incubator (Layer 1) transforms those foundations into technical infrastructure. Concrete applications (Layer 2) put it all in users' hands.
ecosystem.layer0_desc: Metaethical framework, transdisciplinary research, open science publications. Continuing the CIRET with 21st century tools.
ecosystem.layer1_desc: Incubator and lab. EPIS protocol (on-chain epistemic infrastructure), SDK, AI pipelines, standards.
ecosystem.layer2_desc: PICKR, MEMO, TURFu Labs, TCP, TURFurxiv — concrete tools for open research.

cta.title: Contribute
cta.intro: TURFu is an open project. Whether you are a researcher, developer, philosopher, or simply curious — there is a place to contribute.
cta.publish_desc: Propose an article, an analysis, or a research document.
cta.build_desc: Contribute to the code, tools, and the EPIS protocol.
cta.join_desc: Participate in discussions and collective thinking.

visionPage.title: Why TURFu
visionPage.metaDescription: The TURFu thesis: four converging crises, an infrastructure of maturation.
visionPage.intro: We are entering a phase of history where four crises converge without existing institutions being able to respond.
visionPage.crisis_epistemic_body: The systems that produce and validate knowledge — universities, scientific journals, media, expert institutions — are losing legitimacy. Study reproducibility is in freefall (over 70% of psychology results fail to replicate). WEIRD biases (Western, Educated, Industrialized, Rich, Democratic) structure nearly all global psychological research. Traditional peer review, opaque and slow, can no longer keep up with the volume and speed of scientific production. Meanwhile, industrialized disinformation and AI-generated content drown the signal in noise.
visionPage.crisis_coordination_body: Facing systemic problems (climate, inequality, existential risks from AI), inherited governance models — pyramidal, national, slow — show their limits. Global coordination attempts fail where incentive alignment fails. The result: institutions that protect their existence rather than fulfill their function.
visionPage.crisis_sense_body: Civilizational anxiety is not a marginal phenomenon. The loss of shared narratives, combined with unprecedented informational pressure, produces collective disorientation. Neither narrow rationalism nor postmodern relativism provide an operational compass.
visionPage.crisis_tech_body: AI and decentralized technologies (blockchain, DAOs, protocols) are transforming the very nature of proof, reputation, coordination, and trust. But these transformations unfold without a robust ethical framework — or worse, within ad hoc ethical frameworks written after the fact to justify decisions already made.
visionPage.thesis_body:
These four crises are not independent. They are symptoms of the same phenomenon: we have reached a level of systemic complexity that our tools of thought and coordination are no longer capable of handling.

The answer is not another technology, another ideology, or another foundation. It is what we call an infrastructure of maturation — a coherent set of principles, methods, and tools that enable human communities to collectively validate what is true, collectively decide what is just, and collectively build what is viable.

TURFu is the name of this effort. The word comes from French verlan — the inversion of "futur" (future) — and it carries an intention: not to passively accept the future as it arrives by default, but to consciously craft it.
visionPage.concrete_publish: We publish. Transdisciplinary analyses, protocol audits, technical specifications, structured debates. Our corpus is public, versioned, and open to criticism. We are the continuation of CIRET (International Center for Transdisciplinary Research and Studies, founded by Basarab Nicolescu) with 21st century tools.
visionPage.concrete_build: We build tools. The EPIS protocol is an on-chain epistemic infrastructure: every review, every vote, every challenge is verifiable, transparent, resistant to manipulation. PICKR is the first client: any URL on the web can be submitted to specialized AI agent analysis, with results visible alongside human social commentary.
visionPage.concrete_incubate: We incubate projects. Through OZAM (Layer 1), we support aligned initiatives — from open science R&D (TURFu Labs) to content processing tools (TCP) to concrete applications of decentralized coordination.
visionPage.foundations_intro: TURFu is rooted in a precise lineage of thought, not in new age eclecticism:
visionPage.foundations_transdisciplinarity: Transdisciplinarity (Basarab Nicolescu, CIRET) — the recognition that reality possesses multiple levels irreducible to one another, and that knowledge must traverse disciplines rather than remain siloed.
visionPage.foundations_lupasco: The logic of the included middle (Stephane Lupasco) — surpassing binary true/false logic through the recognition of a third term that integrates contradictions at a higher level of reality.
visionPage.foundations_korzybski: General semantics (Alfred Korzybski) — "the map is not the territory". The permanent awareness that our models, our words, our concepts are representations — never reality itself. This principle is operational in everything we build.
visionPage.foundations_wilber: The integral framework (Ken Wilber, AQAL) — the attempt to map the interior/exterior, individual/collective dimensions of every phenomenon. Not as absolute truth, but as a heuristic tool.
visionPage.foundations_field: Lessons from the field — 10 years of experimentation in decentralized social networks (Busy.org on Steem, then Hive), DAOs (Snapshot, on-chain governance), and attempts at collective coordination (La Suite du Monde). These experiences, including their failures, inform every architectural decision we make.
visionPage.not_token: TURFu is not a token. There is no token sale, no speculative tokenomics. The EPIS token will be introduced when the time comes, indexed to utility, with lessons from Steem/Hive applied from day one.
visionPage.not_social: TURFu is not another social network. We build the infrastructure on which epistemic social networks can emerge — that is not the same thing.
visionPage.not_techno: TURFu is not techno-solutionism. The tool alone is not enough. The failures of La Suite du Monde taught us that the blocks are psycho-sociological before being technical. That is why we integrate developmental frameworks (Spiral Dynamics, Kegan) in our approach to onboarding and governance.
visionPage.art_intro: Everything we do follows the A.R.T. framework (Action-Research-Transformation):
visionPage.art_action: Action — we build concrete things (protocols, applications, publications). No theory without implementation.
visionPage.art_research: Research — every action is documented, analyzed, versioned. The audits we publish (Farcaster, PARPA, Lens) are action-research exercises.
visionPage.art_transformation: Transformation — the ultimate goal is not the product but the change it produces: from blind trust to structured verification, from de facto centralization to protocol governance, from disciplinary fragmentation to transdisciplinary thinking.
visionPage.closing: TURFu is not a narrative. It is an infrastructure of maturation.
